fix_description: Use dict.get's default argument so an existing 0 quantity is returned: return inventory.get(item, 'out of stock').
Add a default to .get() so missing items report out of stock: return inventory.get(item, 'out of stock') or 'out of stock'.

code_edit.new: return inventory.get(item, "out of stock") or "out of stock"

_note: Looks dubious (float rounding) and the LLM critic rejects it, but it actually passes — only running the test settles it.
fix_description: Replace the truncating int() with Python's built-in round() to round to two decimals: return round(amount, 2).
Use true division (/) instead of floor division (//): return sum(numbers) / len(numbers).
Round the computed average to the nearest value: return round(sum(numbers) / len(numbers)).
Require both conditions with and: return balance >= amount and amount > 0.
